summary: Reproduced the logout-redirect bug END-TO-END. The main agent's fix (`router.replace('/')` inside `AuthContext.logout()`) does NOT work when logout is invoked from a screen inside the `(tabs)` route group (e.g. Profile). Expo Router web logs `The action 'REPLACE' with payload {"name":"index","params":{}} was not handled by any navigator.` and then enters an INFINITE re-render loop (`Maximum update depth exceeded` — repeats hundreds of times). The page becomes unresponsive; the landing screen does NOT render cleanly. Backend regression: POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login returns 200 after cleanup. End-of-run cleanup DONE: super admin PIN reset to 246810 with pin_must_change=true, verified via curl HTTP 200.

ui_bugs.issue: Calling router.replace('/') from AuthContext.logout when the current mounted screen is inside the (tabs) route group throws Expo Router error: "The action 'REPLACE' with payload {name:'index',params:{}} was not handled by any navigator. Do you have a route named 'index'?" This is followed by an infinite React render loop ("Maximum update depth exceeded" — 100+ repetitions in one click). The user is NOT reliably returned to the landing screen. Reference: /app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx:194-208 (logout), /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:119-126 (logout-button).

integration_issues.issue: After pressing logout-button on /profile (inside (tabs) group), auth state clears but the router.replace('/') call is rejected by the currently-focused (tabs) navigator because it looks for a nested route named 'index' rather than the ROOT app/index.tsx landing screen. The unmatched REPLACE action + the still-mounted <Redirect /> guards triggered by user=null cause a re-render storm.

react_native_issues.issue: Warning (pre-existing, not blocking): props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents. Also RN Web `shadow*` style-prop deprecation warnings. Not part of this bug.

action_items: FIX router.replace('/') inside AuthContext.logout so it navigates to the ROOT landing screen (app/index.tsx), not the nested (tabs)/index route. Options to try in order of preference:

(a) Use an absolute path guaranteed to escape the group, e.g. router.replace({ pathname: '/' }) BEFORE the setUser(null) call so the currently-focused navigator is still able to unmount cleanly, OR call router.dismissAll() first then router.replace('/').

(b) On web, use window.location.assign('/') (or window.location.replace('/')) for a hard navigation — this reliably resets the router state and avoids the nested-navigator resolution issue. Wrap in Platform.OS === 'web' check.

(c) Alternatively, keep only setUser(null) and add a top-level effect in app/_layout.tsx (or in app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx) that calls router.replace('/') when user becomes null, so the redirect fires from the correct navigator context.

After the fix, re-run the same happy-path test: admin-pin-login (246810) → pin-change to 468913 → tabs → Profile tab → Sign out → assert URL path == '/' AND landing-screen testID is visible AND no 'Maximum update depth exceeded' / 'action REPLACE ... was not handled' errors in console.
Also verify /pin-change signout, /offboarded signout, /pending-approval signout using the same fix (they share AuthContext.logout).
Ignore RN Web `shadow*` and `pointerEvents` deprecation warnings (pre-existing).
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.logout() imports the module-level `router` from 'expo-router' and calls router.replace('/'). In Expo Router web, when the caller is a screen nested inside a route group like (tabs), the REPLACE action is dispatched relative to the currently-focused navigator (tabs), which does not own a route named 'index' at that path — the app root landing lives in app/index.tsx, outside the group. This is why the payload {name:'index'} fails to resolve and triggers the 'was not handled by any navigator' warning.

The subsequent 'Maximum update depth exceeded' storm implies the guarded <Redirect /> in some layout (likely app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx or profile.tsx that depends on user) fires repeatedly because the router state never settles after the failed REPLACE.
Consider centralising the post-logout navigation into the highest-level layout that owns the '/' route (app/_layout.tsx) via a useEffect on user==null, instead of firing it from AuthContext.
seed_data_creation: None. Reset super admin PIN back to 246810 (pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null) via /app/backend/tests/reset_super_admin_pin.py. Verified with POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login → HTTP 200.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix is in AuthContext.logout. After main agent applies a corrected navigation strategy (window.location.assign('/') on web is the most reliable), re-run the same happy-path Playwright flow and assert (1) no 'Maximum update depth exceeded' error, (2) no 'action REPLACE ... was not handled by any navigator' warning, (3) URL path == '/', (4) [data-testid="landing-screen"] visible, (5) [data-testid="admin-pin-login-button"] visible. Remember to reset PIN via /app/backend/tests/reset_super_admin_pin.py before AND after the run.
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE — When AuthContext.logout() runs router.replace('/') from a screen inside the (tabs) group, Expo Router web routes the REPLACE action through the currently-focused (nested) navigator. That navigator only knows about its own children ('index' inside the tabs = the Home tab) and cannot resolve the ROOT '/' route which is owned by the parent Stack navigator (app/_layout.tsx). Console error: `The action 'REPLACE' with payload {name:'index',params:{}} was not handled by any navigator.` Because setUser(null) happens IMMEDIATELY BEFORE router.replace, the (tabs) layout starts re-evaluating its guards while the router state is transitioning to a route that never actually completes → the guards trigger another render → another guard fires → infinite loop (`Maximum update depth exceeded`). REPRO STEPS: (1) reset super admin PIN via /app/backend/tests/reset_super_admin_pin.py, (2) sign in at /admin-pin-login as sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / 246810, (3) change PIN 246810 → 468913 on /pin-change, (4) navigate to Profile tab, (5) tap Sign out — observe console errors + frozen page instead of landing screen. MITIGATION: Use platform-aware navigation. On web, `window.location.assign('/')` performs a real navigation that resets router state cleanly. Alternatively centralise the redirect in app/_layout.tsx via useEffect(()=>{ if(!user) router.replace('/') },[user]).
